text: Ṭā, Seen. These are the verses of the Qur’ān [i.e., recitation] and a clear Book
tl: Taa-Seeen; tilka Aayaatul Qur-aani Wa-Kitaabim Mubeen

text: As guidance and good tidings for the believers
tl: Hudanw Wa-bushraa lil-mu'mineen

text: Who establish prayer and give zakāh, and of the Hereafter they are certain [in faith].
tl: Allazeena yuqeemoonas Salaata Wa-yu'toonaz Zakaata Wa-hum bil-Aakhirati hum yooqinoon

text: Indeed, for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have made pleasing to them their deeds, so they wander blindly.
tl: Innal lazeena laa yu'minoona bil-Aakhirati zaiyannaa lahum a'maalahum fahum ya'mahoon

text: Those are the ones for whom there will be the worst of punishment, and in the Hereafter they are the greatest losers.
tl: Ulaaa'ikal lazeena lahum sooo'ul 'azaabi Wa-hum fil Aakhirati humul akhsaroon

text: And indeed, [O Muḥammad], you receive the Qur’ān from one Wise and Knowing.
tl: Wa-innaka latulaqqal Qur-aana mil ladun Hakeemin 'Aleem

text: [Mention] when Moses said to his family, \"Indeed, I have perceived a fire. I will bring you from there information or will bring you a burning torch that you may warm yourselves.\"
tl: Iz qaala Moosaa li-ahliheee inneee aanastu naaran saaateekum minhaa bikhabarin aw aateekum bishihaabin qabasil la'allakum tastaloon

text: But when he came to it, he was called, \"Blessed is whoever is at the fire and whoever is around it. And exalted is Allāh, Lord of the worlds.
tl: Falammaa jaaa'ahaa noodiya am boorika man fin naari Wa-man hawlahaa Wa-Subhaanal laahi Rabbil 'aalameen

text: O Moses, indeed it is I - Allāh, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.\"
tl: Yaa-Moosaaa innahooo Anal laahul 'Azeezul Hakeem

text: And [he was told], \"Throw down your staff.\" But when he saw it writhing as if it were a snake, he turned in flight and did not return. [Allāh said], \"O Moses, fear not. Indeed, in My presence the messengers do not fear.
tl: Wa-alqi 'asaak; falammmaa ra-aahaa tahtazzu ka-annahaa jaaannunw wallaa mudbiranw Wa-lam yu'aqqib; yaa-Moosaa laa takhaf innee laa yakhaafu ladaiyal mursaloon

text: Otherwise, he who wrongs, then substitutes good after evil - indeed, I am Forgiving and Merciful.
tl: Illaa man zalama summa baddala husnam ba'da sooo'in fa-innee Ghafoorur Raheem

text: And put your hand into the opening of your garment [at the breast]; it will come out white without disease. [These are] among the nine signs [you will take] to Pharaoh and his people. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient.\"
tl: Wa-adkhil yadaka fee jaibika takhruj baidaaa'a min ghairi sooo'in fee tis'i Aayaatin ilaa Fir'awna Wa-qawmih; innahum kaanoo qawman faasiqeen

text: But when there came to them Our visible signs, they said, \"This is obvious magic.\"
tl: Falammaa jaaa'at-hum Aayaatunaa mubsiratan qaaloo haazaa sihrum mubeen

text: And they rejected them, while their [inner] selves were convinced thereof, out of injustice and haughtiness. So see how was the end of the corrupters.
tl: Wa-jahadoo bihaa wastaiqanat-haaa anfusuhum zulmanw wa-'uluwwaa; fanzur kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul mufsideen

text: And We had certainly given to David and Solomon knowledge, and they said, \"Praise [is due] to Allāh, who has favored us over many of His believing servants.\"
tl: Wa-laqad aatainaa Daawooda Wa-sulaimaana 'ilmaa; Wa-qaalal hamdu lil-laahil lazee faddalanaa 'alaa kaseerim min 'ibaadihil mu'mineen

text: And Solomon inherited David. He said, \"O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty.\"
tl: Wa-warisa Sulaimaanu Daawooda Wa-qaala yaaa-aiyuhan naasu 'ullimnaa mantiqat tairi Wa-ooteenaa min kulli shai'in inna haazaa lahuwal fadlul mubeen

text: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows
tl: Wa-hushira Sulaimaana junooduhoo minal jinni wal-insi wattairi fahum yooza'oon

text: Until, when they came upon the valley of the ants, an ant said, \"O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Solomon and his soldiers while they perceive not.\"
tl: hattaaa izaaa ataw 'alaa waadin namli qaalat namlatuien yaaa-aiyuhan namlud khuloo masaakinakum laa yahtimannakum Sulaimaanu Wa-junooduhoo Wa-hum laa yash'uroon

text: So [Solomon] smiled, amused at her speech, and said, \"My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve. And admit me by Your mercy into [the ranks of] Your righteous servants.\"
tl: Fatabassama daahikam min qawlihaa Wa-qaala Rabbi awzi'nee an ashkura ni'mata-kal lateee an'amta 'alaiyya Wa-'alaa waalidaiyya Wa-an a'mala saalihan tardaahu Wa-adkhilnee birahmatika fee 'ibaadikas saaliheen

text: And he took attendance of the birds and said, \"Why do I not see the hoopoe - or is he among the absent?
tl: Wa-tafaqqadat taira faqaala maa liya laaa araa al-hudhuda, am kaana minal ghaaa'ibeen

text: I will surely punish him with a severe punishment or slaughter him unless he brings me clear authorization.\"
tl: La-u'azzibanahoo 'azaaban shadeedan aw la-azbahannahoo aw layaatiyannee bisultaanim mubeen

text: But he [i.e., the hoopoe] stayed not long and said, \"I have encompassed [in knowledge] that which you have not encompassed, and I have come to you from Sheba with certain news.
tl: Famakasa ghaira ba'eedin faqaala ahattu bimaa lam tuhit bihee Wa-ji'tuka min Sabaim binaba-iny yaqeen

text: Indeed, I found [there] a woman ruling them, and she has been given of all things, and she has a great throne.
tl: Innee wajattum ra-atan tamlikuhum Wa-ootiyat min kulli shai'inw Wa-lahaa 'arshun 'azeem

text: I found her and her people prostrating to the sun instead of Allāh, and Satan has made their deeds pleasing to them and averted them from [His] way, so they are not guided,
tl: Wajattuhaa Wa-qawmahaa yasjudoona lishshamsi min doonil laahi Wa-zaiyana lahumush Shaitaanu a'maalahum fasaddahum 'anis sabeeli fahum laa yahtadoon

text: [And] so they do not prostrate to Allāh, who brings forth what is hidden within the heavens and the earth and knows what you conceal and what you declare -
tl: Allaa yasjudoo lillaahil lazee yukhrijul khab'a fis samaawaati wal-ardi Wa-ya'lamu maa tukhfoona Wa-maa tu'linoon

text: Allāh - there is no deity except Him, Lord of the Great Throne.\"
tl: Allaahu laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa Rabbul 'Arshil Azeem

text: [Solomon] said, \"We will see whether you were truthful or were of the liars.
tl: Qaala sananzuru asadaqta am kunta minal kaazibeen

text: Take this letter of mine and deliver it to them. Then leave them and see what [answer] they will return.\"
tl: Izhab bikitaabee haaza fa-alqih ilaihim summma tawalla 'anhum fanzur maazaa yarji'oon

text: She said, \"O eminent ones, indeed, to me has been delivered a noble letter.
tl: Qaalat yaaa-aiyyuhal mala'u innee ulqiya ilaiya kitaabun kareem

text: Indeed, it is from Solomon, and indeed, it is [i.e., reads]: 'In the name of Allāh, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful,
tl: Innahoo min Sulaimaana Wa-innahoo bismil laahir Rahmaanir Raheem

text: Be not haughty with me but come to me in submission [as Muslims].'\"
tl: Allaa ta'loo 'alaiya waa-toonee muslimeen

text: She said, \"O eminent ones, advise me in my affair. I would not decide a matter until you witness [for] me.\"
tl: Qaalat yaaa-aiyyuhal mala'u aftoonee fee amree maa kuntu qaati'atan amran hattaa tashhhaddon

text: They said, \"We are men of strength and of great military might, but the command is yours, so see what you will command.\"
tl: Qaaloo nahnu uloo quwwatinw Wa-uloo baasin shadeed; wal-amru ilaiki fanzuree maazaa taamureen

text: She said, \"Indeed kings - when they enter a city, they ruin it and render the honored of its people humbled. And thus do they do.
tl: Qaalat innal mulooka izaa dakhaloo qaryatan afsadoohaa Wa-ja'alooo a'izzata ahlihaaa azillah; Wa-kazaalika yaf'aloon

text: But indeed, I will send to them a gift and see with what [reply] the messengers will return.\"
tl: Wa-innee mursilatun ilaihim bihadiyyatin fanaaziratum bima yarji'ul mursaloon

text: So when they came to Solomon, he said, \"Do you provide me with wealth? But what Allāh has given me is better than what He has given you. Rather, it is you who rejoice in your gift.
tl: Falammaa jaaa'a Sulaimaana qaala atumiddoonani bimaalin famaaa aataaniyal laahu khairum mimmmaaa aataakum bal antum bihadiy-yatikum tafrahoon

text: Return to them, for we will surely come to them with soldiers that they will be powerless to encounter, and we will surely expel them therefrom in humiliation, and they will be debased.\"
tl: Irji' ilaihim falanaatiyan-nahum bijunoodil laa qibala lahum bihaa Wa-lanukhri-jannahum minhaaa azillatanw Wa-hum saaghiroon

text: [Solomon] said, \"O assembly [of jinn], which of you will bring me her throne before they come to me in submission?\"
tl: Qaala yaaa-aiyyuhal mala'u aiyyukum yaateenee bi'arshihaa qabla aien yaatoonee muslimeen

text: A powerful one from among the jinn said, \"I will bring it to you before you rise from your place, and indeed, I am for this [task] strong and trustworthy.\"
tl: Qaala 'ifreetum minal jinni ana aateeka bihee qabla an taqooma mim maqaamika Wa-innee 'alaihi laqawiyyun ameen

text: Said one who had knowledge from the Scripture, \"I will bring it to you before your glance returns to you.\" And when [Solomon] saw it placed before him, he said, \"This is from the favor of my Lord to test me whether I will be grateful or ungrateful. And whoever is grateful - his gratitude is only for [the benefit of] himself. And whoever is ungrateful - then indeed, my Lord is Free of need and Generous.\"
tl: Qaalal lazee indahoo 'ilmum minal Kitaabi ana aateeka bihee qabla aien yartadda ilaika tarfuk; falammaa ra-aahu mustaqirran 'indahoo qaala haazaa min fadli Rabbee li-yabluwaneee 'a-ashkuru am akfuru Wa-man shakara fa-innamaa yashkuru linafsihee Wa-man kafara fa-inna Rabbee Ghaniyyun Kareem

text: He said, \"Disguise for her her throne; we will see whether she will be guided [to truth] or will be of those who is not guided.\"
tl: Qaala nakkiroo lahaa 'arshahaa nanzur atahtadeee am takoonu minal lazeena laa yahtadoon

text: So when she arrived, it was said [to her], \"Is your throne like this?\" She said, \"[It is] as though it was it.\" [Solomon said], \"And we were given knowledge before her, and we have been Muslims [in submission to Allāh].
tl: Falammaa jaaa'at qeela ahaakaza 'arshuki qaalat kaanna hoo; Wa-ooteenal 'ilma min qablihaa Wa-kunnaa muslimeen

text: And that which she was worshipping other than Allāh had averted her [from submission to Him]. Indeed, she was from a disbelieving people.\"
tl: Wa-saddahaa maa kaanat ta'budu min doonil laahi innahaa kaanat min qawmin kaafireen

text: She was told, \"Enter the palace.\" But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins [to wade through]. He said, \"Indeed, it is a palace [whose floor is] made smooth with glass.\" She said, \"My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allāh, Lord of the worlds.\"
tl: Qeela lahad khulis sarha falammaa ra-at-hu hasibat-hu lujjatanw Wa-khashafat 'an saaqaihaa; qaala innahoo sarhum mumarradum min qawaareer; qaalat Rabbi innee zalamtu nafsee Wa-aslamtu ma'a Sulaimaana lillaahi Rabbil 'aalameen

text: And We had certainly sent to Thamūd their brother Ṣāliḥ, [saying], \"Worship Allāh,\" and at once they were two parties conflicting.
tl: Wa laqad arsalnaaa ilaa Samooda akhaahum Saalihan ani'budul lahha fa-izaa hum fareeqaani yakhtasimoon

text: He said, \"O my people, why are you impatient for evil before [i.e., instead of] good? Why do you not seek forgiveness of Allāh that you may receive mercy?\"
tl: Qaala yaa-qawmi lima tasta'jiloona bissaiyi'ati qablal hasanati law-laa tas-taghfiroonal laaha la'allakum turhamoon

text: They said, \"We consider you a bad omen, you and those with you.\" He said, \"Your omen [i.e., fate] is with Allāh. Rather, you are a people being tested.\"
tl: Qaalut taiyyarnaa bika Wa-bimam ma'ak; qaala taaa'irukum 'indal laahi bal antum qawmun tuftanoon

text: And there were in the city nine family heads causing corruption in the land and not amending [its affairs].
tl: Wa-kaana fil madeenati tis'atu rahtien yufsidoona fil ardi Wa-laa yuslihoon

text: They said, \"Take a mutual oath by Allāh that we will kill him by night, he and his family. Then we will say to his executor, 'We did not witness the destruction of his family, and indeed, we are truthful.'\"
tl: Qaaloo taqaasamoo billaahi lanubaiyyitannahoo Wa-ahlahoo summaa lanaqoolana liwaliy-yihee maa shahidnaa mahlika ahliee Wa-innaa lasaadiqoon

text: And they planned a plan, and We planned a plan, while they perceived not.
tl: Wa-makaroo makranw Wa-makarnaa makranw Wa-hum laa yash'uroon

text: Then look how was the outcome of their plan - that We destroyed them and their people, all.
tl: Fanzur kaifa kaana 'aaqibatu makrihim annaa dammar-naahum Wa-qawmahum ajma'een

text: So those are their houses, desolate because of the wrong they had done. Indeed in that is a sign for people who know.
tl: Fatilka buyootuhum khaa-wiyatam bimaa zalamoo; inna fee zaalika la-Aayatal liqaw-mien ya'lamoon

text: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allāh.
tl: Wa-anjainal lazeena aamanoo Wa-kaanoo yattaqoon

text: And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, \"Do you commit immorality while you are seeing?
tl: Wa-lootan iz qaala liqawmiheee ataatoonal faa-hishata Wa-antum tubsiroon

text: Do you indeed approach men with desire instead of women? Rather, you are a people behaving ignorantly.\"
tl: A'innakum lataatoonar rijaala shahwatam min doonin nisaaa'; bal antum qawmun tajhaloon

text: But the answer of his people was not except that they said, \"Expel the family of Lot from your city. Indeed, they are people who keep themselves pure.\"
tl: Famaa kaana jawaaba qawmiheee illaaa an qaalooo akhrijooo aalaa Lootim min qaryatikum innahum unaasuny yatatahharoon

text: So We saved him and his family, except for his wife; We destined her to be of those who remained behind.
tl: Fa-anjainaahu Wa-ahlahooo illam ra-atahoo qaddarnaahaa minal ghaabireen

text: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
tl: Wa-amtarnaa 'alaihimm mataran fasaaa'a matarul munzareen

text: Say, [O Muḥammad], \"Praise be to Allāh, and peace upon His servants whom He has chosen. Is Allāh better or what they associate with Him?\"
tl: Qulil hamdu lillaahi Wa-salaamun 'alaa 'ibaadihil lazeenas tafaa; aaallaahu khairun ammmaa yushrikoon

text: [More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allāh? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him].
tl: Amman khalaqas samaawaati wal-arda Wa-anzala lakum minas samaaa'i maaa'an fa-anbatnaa bihee hadaaa'iqa zaata bahjah; maa kaana lakum an tunbitoo shajarahaa; 'a-ilaahun ma'al laah; bal hum qawmuien ya'diloon

text: Is He [not best] who made the earth a stable ground and placed within it rivers and made for it firmly set mountains and placed between the two seas a barrier? Is there a deity with Allāh? [No], but most of them do not know.
tl: Ammann ja'alal arda qaraaranw Wa-ja'ala khilaalahaaa anhaaranw Wa-ja'ala lahaa rawaasiya Wa-ja'ala bainal bahraini haajizaa; 'a-ilaahun ma' allah; bal aksaruhum laa ya'lamoon

text: Is He [not best] who responds to the desperate one when he calls upon Him and removes evil and makes you inheritors of the earth? Is there a deity with Allāh? Little do you remember.
tl: Ammaien yujeebul mud-tarra izaa da'aahu Wa-yakshifus sooo'a Wa-yaj'alukum khula-faaa'al ardi 'a-ilaahun ma' allaah qaleelan maa tazak-karoon

text: Is He [not best] who guides you through the darknesses of the land and sea and who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy? Is there a deity with Allāh? High is Allāh above whatever they associate with Him.
tl: Ammaien yahdeekum fee zulumaatil barri wal bahri Wa-maien yursilu riyaaha bushran baina yadai rahmatih; 'a-ilaahun ma'allah; Ta'aalal laahu 'ammaa yushrikoon

text: Is He [not best] who begins creation and then repeats it and who provides for you from the heaven and earth? Is there a deity with Allāh? Say, \"Produce your proof, if you should be truthful.\"
tl: Ammaien yabda'ul khalqa thumma yu'eeduhoo Wa-maien yarzuqukum minas sammaaa'i wal ard; 'a-ilaahun ma'allah; qul haatoo burhaanakum in kuntum saadiqeen

text: Say, \"None in the heavens and earth knows the unseen except Allāh, and they do not perceive when they will be resurrected.\"
tl: Qul laa ya'lamu man fis sammaawaati wal-ardil ghaiba illal laah; Wa-maa yash'uroona aiyaana yub'asoon

text: Rather, their knowledge is arrested concerning the Hereafter. Rather, they are in doubt about it. Rather, they are, concerning it, blind.
tl: Balid daaraka 'ilmuhum fil Aakhirah; bal hum fee shakkin minhaa bal hum minhaa 'amoon

text: And those who disbelieve say, \"When we have become dust as well as our forefathers, will we indeed be brought out [of the graves]?
tl: Wa-qaalal lazeena kafarooo 'a-izaa kunnaa turaabanw Wa-aabaaa'unaaa a'innaa lamukhrajoon

text: We have been promised this, we and our forefathers, before. This is not but legends of the former peoples.\"
tl: Laqad wu'idnaa haazaa nahnu Wa-aabaaa'unaa min qablu in haazaaa illaaa asaateerul awwaleen

text: Say, [O Muḥammad], \"Proceed [i.e., travel] through the land and observe how was the end of the criminals.\"
tl: Qul seeroo fil ardi fanzuroo kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul mujrimeen

text: And grieve not over them or be in distress from what they conspire.
tl: Wa-laa tahzan 'alaihim Wa-laa takun fee daiqin mimmaa yamkuroon

text: And they say, \"When is [the fulfillment of] this promise, if you should be truthful?\"
tl: Wa-yaqooloona mataa haazal wa'du in kuntum saadiqeen

text: Say, \"Perhaps it is close behind you [i.e., very near] - some of that for which you are impatient.
tl: Qul 'asaaa aien yakoona radifa lakum ba'dul lazee tasta'jiloon

text: And indeed, your Lord is the possessor of bounty for the people, but most of them are not grateful.\"
tl: Wa-inna Rabbaka lazoo fadlin 'alan naasi Wa-laakinna aksarahum laa yashkuroon

text: And indeed, your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.
tl: Wa-inna Rabbaka la-ya'lamu maa tukinnu sudooruhum Wa-maa yu'linoon

text: And there is nothing concealed within the heaven and the earth except that it is in a clear Register.
tl: Wa-maa min ghaaa'ibatin fis samaaa'i wal-ardi illaa fee kitaabin mubeen

text: Indeed, this Qur’ān relates to the Children of Israel most of that over which they disagree.
tl: Inna haazal Qur-aana yaqussu 'alaa Baneee israaa'eela aksaral lazee hum feehi yakhtalifoon

text: And indeed, it is guidance and mercy for the believers.
tl: Wa-innahoo lahudanw Wa-rahmatul lilmu'mineen

text: Indeed, your Lord will judge between them by His [wise] judgement. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
tl: Inna Rabbaka yaqdee bainahum bihukmih; Wa-Huwal 'Azeezul 'Aleem

text: So rely upon Allāh; indeed, you are upon the clear truth.
tl: Fatawakkal 'alal laahi innaka 'alal haqqil mubeen

text: Indeed, you will not make the dead hear, nor will you make the deaf hear the call when they have turned their backs retreating.
tl: Innaka laa tusmi'ul mawtaa Wa-laa tusmi'us summad du'aaa izaa wallaw mudbireen

text: And you cannot guide the blind away from their error. You will only make hear those who believe in Our verses so they are Muslims [i.e., submitting to Allāh].
tl: Wa-maaa anta bihaadil-'umyi 'an dalaalatihim in tusmi'u illaa mai yu'minu bi aayaatinaa fahum muslimoon

text: And when the word [i.e., decree] befalls them, We will bring forth for them a creature from the earth speaking to them, [saying] that the people were, of Our verses, not certain [in faith].
tl: Wa-izaa waqa'al qawlu 'alaihim akhrajnaa lahum daaabbatan minal ardi tukal-limuhum annan naasa kaanoo bi-aayaatinaa laa yooqinoon

text: And [warn of] the Day when We will gather from every nation a company of those who deny Our signs, and they will be [driven] in rows
tl: Wa-Yawma nahshuru min kulli ummatin fawjan mimmaien yukazzibu bi-Aayaatinaa fahum yooza'oon

text: Until, when they arrive [at the place of Judgement], He will say, \"Did you deny My signs while you encompassed them not in knowledge, or what [was it that] you were doing?\"
tl: Hattaaa izaa jaaa'oo qaala akazzabtum bi-Aayaatee Wa-lam tuheetoo bihaa 'ilman ammaazaa kuntum ta'maloon

text: And the decree will befall them for the wrong they did, and they will not [be able to] speak.
tl: Wa-waqa'al qawlu 'alaihim bimaa zalamoo fahum laa yantiqoon

text: Do they not see that We made the night that they may rest therein and the day giving sight? Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.
tl: Alam yaraw annaa ja'alnal laila li-yaskunoo feehi wannahaara mubsiraa; inna fee zaalika la-Aayaatil liqaw-mien yu'minoon

text: And [warn of] the Day the Horn will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth will be terrified except whom Allāh wills. And all will come to Him humbled.
tl: Wa-Yawma yunfakhu fis Soori fafazi'a man fis samaawaati Wa-man fil ardi illaa man shaaa'al laah; Wa-kullun atawhu daakhireen

text: And you see the mountains, thinking them motionless, while they will pass as the passing of clouds. [It is] the work of Allāh, who perfected all things. Indeed, He is Aware of that which you do.
tl: Wa-taral jibaala tahsabuhaa jaamidatanw Wa-hiya tamurru marras sahaab; sun'al laahil lazeee atqana kulla shai'; innahoo khabeerun bimaa taf'aloon

text: Whoever comes [at Judgement] with a good deed will have better than it, and they, from the terror of that Day, will be safe.
tl: Man jaaa'a bil-hasanati falahoo khairun minhaa Wa-hum min faza'ien Yawma'izin aaminoon

text: And whoever comes with an evil deed - their faces will be overturned into the Fire, [and it will be said], \"Are you recompensed except for what you used to do?\"
tl: Wa-man jaaa'a bissai-yi'ati fakubbat wujoohuhum fin Naari hal tujzawna illaa maa kuntum ta'maloon

text: [Say, O Muḥammad], \"I have only been commanded to worship the Lord of this city, who made it sacred and to whom [belongs] all things. And I am commanded to be of the Muslims [i.e., those who submit to Allāh]
tl: Innamaaa umirtu an a'buda Rabba haazihil baldatil lazee harramahaa Wa-lahoo kullu shai'inw Wa-umirtu an akoona minal muslimeen

text: And to recite the Qur’ān.\" And whoever is guided is only guided for [the benefit of] himself; and whoever strays - say, \"I am only [one] of the warners.\"
tl: Wa-an atluwal Qur-aana famanih tadaa fa-innamaa yahtadee linafsihee Wa-man dalla faqul innamaaa ana minal munzireen

text: And say, \"[All] praise is [due] to Allāh. He will show you His signs, and you will recognize them. And your Lord is not unaware of what you do.\"
tl: Wa-qulil hamdu lillaahi sa-yureekum Aayaatihee fa-ta'rifoonahaa; Wa-maa Rabbuka bighaafilin 'ammaa ta'maloon
